title: Large World Cup tent on market square for sixth time: “Winning match against Brazil in 2018 was our best night ever”

news.title: Large World Cup tent on market square for sixth time: “Winning match against Brazil in 2018 was our best night ever”

world.title: Large World Cup tent on market square for sixth time: “Winning match against Brazil in 2018 was our best night ever”

environment.compositionIntent: Hans Hartung-style all-over pressure field: pale paper/ochre ground, off-left knot, lateral black band, right vertical grid barricade, muted green dot catches, and active negative space translating Against/market-square World Cup crowd pressure.

distanceSafety.reason: Attempt 2 desktop was readable, but mobile still held the right vertical barricade mostly at the edge; final camera is slightly farther back/right so portrait framing includes clearer barricade pressure while retaining the off-left knot and lateral band.

contrast.notes: Warm matte light keeps paper/ochre negative space open while a muted green rim separates the vertical barricade and side-return marks.

params.compositionUse: Full surrounding paper/ochre and muted green field; rooted at origin to preserve accepted environment shell behavior.

selfReview.summary: Accepted: real WebGPU-safe Three.js world-environment module with a single surrounding direction-space shell plus bounded standard-material geometry for Hartung/news-specific pressure systems.
strengths: Uses the correct packet identity, Hans Hartung source facts, and selected World Cup tent news meaning.
Avoids ShaderMaterial, RawShaderMaterial, raw GLSL, onBeforeCompile, network, remote assets, and CPU-baked textures.
Provides a dominant enclosing painted surround, not a neutral backdrop.
Preview evidence proves actual WebGPU backend and successful health/capture gate.
concerns: First two attempts failed the environment coverage heuristic; final attempt corrected this with stronger all-over wash coverage.

evidence.compositionUse: Used as the matte all-over field and far pressure scaffold.
compositionAdaptations: Kept rooted at origin as the full matte paper, ochre, muted green environment shell.
composition modified world-environment from isolated accepted-part defaults

params.compositionUse: Primary Against/news signal, final-scaled so mobile and desktop both carry a readable knot, sweeping lateral band, and right vertical barricade.

strengths: Readable in isolation at desktop preview scale.
Preserves abstract-no-subject composition through all-over marks instead of a centered object.
Uses WebGPU-safe standard materials and explicit materialFactoryId evidence.
Carries the required off-center knot, lateral band, dense vertical barricade, active pale negative space, grids, dots, and high-contrast edges.
concerns: The isolated preview includes some dark default environment visible behind the ordinary geometry, but the authored pale fields dominate the primary read and the render health gate passed.

evidence.compositionUse: Attempt 3 keeps the accepted primary signal foregrounded while reducing crowding and improving portrait visibility of the required grid-pressure barricade.
compositionAdaptations: Scaled to 0.68 and kept foreground so knot, lateral band, and vertical barricade remain readable across desktop and mobile.
composition modified non-literal-grid-pressure from isolated accepted-part defaults

params.compositionUse: Secondary Hartung line network, right/back-scaled to brace the barricade and add drips/dot catches without competing as a central subject.

selfReview.summary: Accepted: the draft is a real deterministic Three.js module with preview-backed WebGPU evidence and visible Hartung-style mark logic for artist-mark-field.
strengths: Strong off-center knot and sweeping black trajectories.
Readable non-literal grid pressure with vertical barricade and ordered dot catches.
WebGPU-safe standard materials and explicit materialFactoryId evidence only.
concerns: Large translucent wash planes read intentionally flat and graphic; acceptable for this artist-style-system part but should be balanced by the accepted environment and primary-news-signal parts in full composition.

evidence.compositionUse: Attempt 3 uses this layer as right-side support and atmospheric line pressure.
compositionAdaptations: Moved right/back and scaled to 0.44 to brace the barricade without over-darkening the primary knot.
composition modified artist-mark-field from isolated accepted-part defaults

compositionEvidence.summary: Final revision: preview-driven camera and scale adjustment preserves all accepted WebGPU-compatible parts while making the abstract pressure system readable in desktop and portrait.
decisions: Attempt 1 succeeded technically but mobile cropped most of the required right vertical barricade, so the canonical camera was adjusted for an evidence-backed artwork failure rather than portrait styling alone.

Attempt 2 restored some barricade visibility; attempt 3 moved the camera slightly farther back/right and reduced primary scale to make the knot, lateral band, and vertical barricade all readable in mobile.

The foreground grid-pressure part remains the primary news signal; the artist-mark-field is smaller and farther back/right so it braces the vertical pressure instead of over-blackening the off-left knot.
All moduleRef, assetRefs, rendererCompatibility evidenceReasons, compatibleSurfaces, and webgpuFeatureFacts are preserved from the accepted packaged parts.
Final output grading stays renderer-owned and subtle: contrast/saturation/exposure only, with no WebGL-only post effects or shader assumptions.

image.altText: Large World Cup tent on market square for sixth time: “Winning match against Brazil in 2018 was our best night ever” by Hans Hartung.
